bio.bio_blurb: Her painted portrait survives in a Vermont museum

narrative_blocks.header: Her painted portrait survives in Vermont
narrative_blocks.body: A portrait of Abby, painted in oil about 1840 by Benjamin Franklin Mason, is held today by the Henry Sheldon Museum of Vermont History and cataloged by the National Portrait Gallery.

bio.bio_blurb: Troy and Middlebury merchant; county clerk

narrative_blocks.body: Emma died in 1840, aged about fourteen. Emma’s parents gave the name to a child born two years later.

bio.bio_blurb: Lawyer and 34-year clerk of Addison County Court

narrative_blocks.header: He kept the county court for thirty-four years
narrative_blocks.body: A Middlebury College lawyer, Rufus Wainwright Jr. served as Clerk of the Addison County Court from 1870 until his death in 1904 — an office his son would be called home to fill after him.

cross_connections.display_label: was his first cousin; their children married.

bio.bio_blurb: Dry-goods merchant of the Beckwith & Co. block

narrative_blocks.header: His firm built the county’s finest block
narrative_blocks.body: With his brother-in-law Smith Beckwith, Gardner Wainwright ran the mercantile house of Beckwith & Co., which in 1883 raised one of the finest business blocks in the county; he later remade Judge Painter’s old residence into one of Middlebury’s grandest homes.

bio.bio_blurb: Boston-born; wife of merchant Buel Beckwith
Lost in a South American war
Grist miller and bootmaker who went west

narrative_blocks.body: Samuel died in 1826, aged about four.

cross_connections.display_label: was her first cousin; their children married.

bio.bio_blurb: Civil War sergeant of the 169th New York

military_service.notes: Enlisted Aug 1862 at Troy as sergeant; promoted commissary sergeant Aug 1864; mustered out July 1865 at Raleigh, NC.

narrative_blocks.header: From Troy to Raleigh with the 169th
narrative_blocks.body: Samuel Charles Sargeant enlisted at Troy in 1862, served three years with Company G of the 169th New York Infantry, rose to commissary sergeant, and mustered out at Raleigh at the war’s end.
Emma died in 1851, aged about four.

bio.bio_blurb: Merchant and hotel owner in Rolfe, Iowa

burialCemetery.name: Middlebury Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Middlebury Cemetery, Middlebury Addison Co VT. Edward Payson Hooker family + David G. Hooker's first wife. Hooker tree: Sarah Parker Harris Hooker (X-prefix, David G's first wife, d.1868 age 30); Edward Clarendon Hooker (H03917, m. Ruth Fairchild); Emily Griswold Hooker (H03916).
